summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by reading from 2 Kings 6, where a prophet's disciple loses a borrowed ax head in the Jordan River, and Elisha miraculously retrieves it. While acknowledging several potential sermon angles from the story, Pastor Travis explained that God led him to focus on the opening verses, where the disciples tell Elisha, 'The place where we are staying is too small for us.' From this, he delivered the central message: 'You've outgrown that.' He challenged the congregation to recognize areas in their lives where they have spiritually outgrown old mindsets, environments, and behaviors, emphasizing that God calls believers to move from spiritual infancy to maturity. The sermon focused on two key areas we must outgrow: our speech and our thoughts. Pastor Travis taught that mature Christians must put away childish, negative, and unwholesome talk, understanding the immense power of our words to bring life or death. He then connected our speech to our thought life, explaining that our words are the fruit of the seeds planted in our minds. He urged a renewal of the mind through Scripture and the Holy Spirit to break habitual negative thinking. The message culminated in a call to spiritual growth, inviting listeners to dream bigger for God's kingdom and to confess Jesus as Lord, using the power of our words for salvation and transformation.
mainPoints: God is calling believers to recognize they have spiritually outgrown old mindsets, environments, and childish behaviors.
Spiritual maturity requires putting away unwholesome speech and understanding the life-giving or destructive power of our words.
Renewing our thought patterns through God's Word is essential, as our words and lives are shaped by what we think.

versesMentioned.text: The company of the prophets said to Elisha, 'Look, the place where we meet with you is too small for us. Let us go to the Jordan, where each of us can get a pole; and let us build a place there for us to meet.' And he said, 'Go.' Then one of them said, 'Won’t you please come with your servants?' 'I will,' Elisha replied. And he went with them. They went to the Jordan and began to cut down trees. As one of them was cutting down a tree, the iron axhead fell into the water. 'Oh no, my lord!' he cried out. 'It was borrowed!' The man of God asked, 'Where did it fall?' When he showed him the place, Elisha cut a stick and threw it there, and made the iron float. 'Lift it out,' he said. Then the man reached out his hand and took it.

The Lord will make you the head, not the tail. If you pay attention to the commands of the Lord your God that I give you this day and carefully follow them, you will always be at the top, never at the bottom.
Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? Yet not one of them is forgotten by God. Indeed, the very hairs of your head are all numbered. Don’t be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.
Therefore let us move beyond the elementary teachings about Christ and be taken forward to maturity, not laying again the foundation of repentance from acts that lead to death, and of faith in God.
In fact, though by this time you ought to be teachers, you need someone to teach you the elementary truths of God’s word all over again. You need milk, not solid food!
Set a guard over my mouth, Lord; keep watch over the door of my lips.
The tongue has the power of life and death, and those who love it will eat its fruit.
Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.
Nor should there be obscenity, foolish talk or coarse joking, which are out of place, but rather thanksgiving.
Instead, speaking the truth in love, we will grow to become in every respect the mature body of him who is the head, that is, Christ.

We all stumble in many ways. Anyone who is never at fault in what they say is perfect, able to keep their whole body in check. When we put bits into the mouths of horses to make them obey us, we can turn the whole animal. Or take ships as an example. Although they are so large and are driven by strong winds, they are steered by a very small rudder wherever the pilot wants to go. Likewise, the tongue is a small part of the body, but it makes great boasts. Consider what a great forest is set on fire by a small spark. The tongue also is a fire, a world of evil among the parts of the body. It corrupts the whole body, sets the whole course of one’s life on fire, and is itself set on fire by hell. All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles and sea creatures are being tamed and have been tamed by mankind, but no human being can tame the tongue. It is a restless evil, full of deadly poison. With the tongue we praise our Lord and Father, and with it we curse human beings, who have been made in God’s likeness. Out of the same mouth come praise and cursing. My brothers and sisters, this should not be. Can both fresh water and salt water flow from the same spring? My brothers and sisters, can a fig tree bear olives, or a grapevine bear figs? Neither can a salt spring produce fresh water.
But I tell you that everyone will have to give account on the day of judgment for every empty word they have spoken.
You brood of vipers, how can you who are evil say anything good? For the mouth speaks what the heart is full of.
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.
Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.

You were taught, with regard to your former way of life, to put off your old self, which is being corrupted by its deceitful desires; to be made new in the attitude of your minds; and to put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.

Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, 'Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory! Let your hand be with me, and keep me from harm so that I will be free from pain.' And God granted his request.
If you declare with your mouth, 'Jesus is Lord,' and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved.

Monday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 13:11 - 'When I was a child, I talked like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child. When I became a man, I put the ways of childhood behind me.'
Monday.action: Prayerfully ask God to show you one specific 'childish' mindset or habit you have outgrown and need to put behind you.
Monday.prayer: Lord, reveal to me where I have stayed small in my thinking or behavior. Give me the courage to put those childish ways behind me today.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 141:3 - 'Set a guard over my mouth, Lord; keep watch over the door of my lips.'
Tuesday.action: Be intentional about speaking only words that are helpful and build others up. If you catch yourself about to gossip or complain, pause and choose silence or encouragement instead.
Tuesday.prayer: God, set a guard over my mouth today. Help my words bring life, not death, to everyone I interact with.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 - 'Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.'
Wednesday.action: Take a 15-minute break from social media or news. Use that time to read a Psalm or listen to worship music instead.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I guard my heart by turning to You. Renew my mind and wash away negative thought patterns. Let my heart be a wellspring of life.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 4:29 - 'Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.'
Thursday.focus: Building others up with our words.
Thursday.action: Intentionally encourage at least two people today with specific, sincere words of affirmation.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide my conversations. Let my words be a source of strength and benefit to everyone who hears them.

Friday.scripture: Romans 12:2 - 'Do not conform to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind.'
Friday.action: Memorize and meditate on one of the verses from this sermon (e.g., 1 Chronicles 4:10 or Philippians 4:8). Write it down and reflect on it throughout the day.
Friday.prayer: God, transform me by renewing my mind. Help me to think Your thoughts and see myself and my future through Your eyes of faith.

Saturday.scripture: 1 Chronicles 4:10 - 'Jabez cried out to the God of Israel, "Oh, that you would bless me and enlarge my territory!"'
Saturday.action: Spend time in prayer, asking God to 'enlarge your territory'—not for selfish gain, but for greater impact and generosity for His kingdom. Write down one God-sized dream He places on your heart.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, like Jabez, I ask You to bless me and enlarge my territory. Stretch my faith, expand my influence, and use me for Your glory. I trust You with bigger dreams.
